fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Illinois Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity illinois, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to illinois, respiratory therapist compact illinois, illinois respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Illinois IDFPR — Respiratory Care
board.parent_dept: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)
board.mailing_address:
Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
ATTN: Division of Professional Regulation
320 W. Washington, 3rd Floor
Springfield, IL 62786

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) exam

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Illinois.

steps.title: Create an IDFPR Online Services Portal Account
steps.description: Visit the IDFPR Online Services Portal at https://www.idfpr.com/Default.asp?redirect=true and register as a new user if you do not already have an IDFPR license or account. You will need to verify your email and set up security questions.

steps.title: Complete and Submit the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your IDFPR Online Services Portal account, select 'Apply for a new license', and complete the Respiratory Care Practitioner application. You will need to provide personal details, educational history, and attestations regarding child support and student loans.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your accredited respiratory care program to send official transcripts directly to the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR). The transcripts must confirm completion of a program meeting Illinois requirements.

steps.title: Provide NBRC RRT Credential Verification
steps.description: Ensure the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) verifies your Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT) credential directly to IDFPR, confirming you have passed the required examination.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee of $20.00 through the IDFPR Online Services Portal during the application process. This fee covers initial licensure or licensure by endorsement.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Illinois respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Illinois have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Illinois?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Illinois
hero.dek: To obtain your Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Illinois, you must apply online through the IDFPR Online Services Portal.

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the IDFPR Online Services Portal.
2. Click 'New User? Register Now' to create an account.
3. Follow the prompts to provide personal information, verify your email address, and set up security questions.
4. Once your account is successfully created and verified, log in.
5. Select 'Apply for a new license' to begin your Respiratory Care Practitioner application.
